failingTest: getTagDef('PROSODY') returns undefined instead of prosody definition
fileMap: apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts — modify — case-insensitive comparison in getTagDef()
apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts — modify — add case-insensitive lookup tests
fixApproach: In getTagDef(), normalize the input tagName to lowercase before comparing against the tag definitions (all stored lowercase). Change line 195 from `t.tag === tagName` to `t.tag === tagName.toLowerCase()`. This fixes isTagSupportedByProvider() automatically. Add 6 new test cases covering uppercase, mixed-case, lowercase, nonexistent, and cross-provider parity.
steps: Add case-insensitive lookup tests to apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.test.ts (uppercase, mixed-case, lowercase, nonexistent, and isTagSupportedByProvider parity checks)
Change getTagDef() in apps/web/src/lib/ssml/tags.ts to compare lowercased tag names (tagName.toLowerCase())
Run npm run test to verify all existing and new tests pass
summary: Make getTagDef() case-insensitive by lowercasing input tagName before comparison
